title: CHAPTER 15 —
SPECIALIZED AND FINANCIAL CHARTS
segments.body_html:
The candlestick convention earns its strangeness because three conditions hold simultaneously. The audience has already

Chapter 15 — Specialized and Financial C — Take those three conditions away and you get decorative noise. A gauge chart on a dashboard looks like an instrument pan

beats.text: The candlestick convention earns its strangeness because three conditions hold simultaneously. The audience has already learned it (financial graphicacy is widespread among traders and analysts). The encoding is perceptually honest (position is used throughout; color encodes direction, not magnitude). And the form encodes something genuinely harder to show in a standard chart , four time-series in a single glyph without any visual compression cheating. Take those three conditions away and you get decorative noise. A gauge chart on a dashboard looks like an instrument panel from a cockpit.

segments.label: 01 / 13 · HOOK

title: CANDLESTICK AND
OHLC CHARTS
segments.body_html:
The candlestick is the standard form for financial price visualization because it uses position throughout. The body's h

Candlestick and OHLC charts — The OHLC chart is the older variant. Instead of a candle body, it uses a vertical bar spanning the full high-to-low rang

beats.text: The candlestick is the standard form for financial price visualization because it uses position throughout. The body's height is the distance from open to close , a length measurement, channel rank two. The wick heights are position from the body edge to the extreme price , also length measurements. The color is a binary direction indicator, not a magnitude channel. The OHLC chart is the older variant.

segments.label: 02 / 13 · CANDLESTICK AND OHLC CHARTS

title: KAGI AND POINT & FIGURE:
REMOVING TIME FROM THE AXIS
segments.body_html:
Candlestick and OHLC charts have time on the x-axis. Each candle occupies one period of equal width, whether the period

Kagi and Point & Figure: removing time f — A Kagi chart has a price line that moves up when prices rise and down when they fall. The line does not advance along th

beats.text: Candlestick and OHLC charts have time on the x-axis. Each candle occupies one period of equal width, whether the period was eventful or quiet. Kagi and Point and Figure charts remove time from the axis entirely. A Kagi chart has a price line that moves up when prices rise and down when they fall. The line does not advance along the x-axis based on elapsed time. It advances based on price movement.

segments.label: 03 / 13 · KAGI AND POINT & FIGURE: REMOV

segments.section: BULLET GRAPHS AND THE GAUGE CH
title: BULLET GRAPHS AND
THE GAUGE CHART REPLACEMENT
segments.body_html:
The bullet graph is a dashboard-design intervention. Stephen Few proposed it as a direct replacement for the gauge chart

Bullet graphs and the gauge chart replac — The bullet graph has three components. The primary measure is a solid horizontal bar from zero to the current value. A t

beats.text: The bullet graph is a dashboard-design intervention. Stephen Few proposed it as a direct replacement for the gauge chart, which was (and still is) the dominant form for showing a single performance metric against a target in dashboard software. The bullet graph has three components. The primary measure is a solid horizontal bar from zero to the current value. A target marker , a short vertical tick , sits at the target value.

segments.label: 04 / 13 · BULLET GRAPHS AND THE GAUGE CH

title: RADAR CHARTS AND
THE AXIS-ORDER PROBLEM
segments.body_html:
A radar chart places multiple variables as axes radiating from a center. Each observation is a polygon connecting its va

Radar charts and the axis-order problem — The form seems to invite multi-dimensional comparison. In practice it introduces a specific channel failure that most of

beats.text: A radar chart places multiple variables as axes radiating from a center. Each observation is a polygon connecting its values along these axes. For five variables per observation and four observations, you get four overlapping polygons on a five-spoked wheel. The form seems to invite multi-dimensional comparison. In practice it introduces a specific channel failure that most of its users don't notice.

segments.label: 05 / 13 · RADAR CHARTS AND THE AXIS-ORDE

title: POLAR AREA CHARTS (A
BRIEF RETURN).
segments.body_html:
The Nightingale rose chart reappears here because it is sometimes treated as a "specialized" form for cyclical data — mo

Polar area charts (a brief return) — When the data is genuinely cyclic — twelve months of the year, twenty-four hours of the day, seven days of the week — th

beats.text: The Nightingale rose chart reappears here because it is sometimes treated as a "specialized" form for cyclical data , monthly patterns arranged radially around a clock face. The polar area encoding was discussed in Chapter 11 in the context of rhetorical vs. analytical contexts; the same analysis applies here. When the data is genuinely cyclic , twelve months of the year, twenty-four hours of the day, seven days of the week , the radial layout can reinforce the cyclic structure.

title: WHEN SPECIALIZED CHARTS BECOME
DECORATIVE NOISE.
segments.body_html:
The connecting thread across all the failures in this chapter is the same: a specialized form used for its appearance ra

When specialized charts become decorativ — Gauge charts look like professional instruments. They appear in enterprise dashboards because dashboard-software default

beats.text: The connecting thread across all the failures in this chapter is the same: a specialized form used for its appearance rather than for the specific analytical question it was designed to answer. Gauge charts look like professional instruments. They appear in enterprise dashboards because dashboard-software defaults include them, because they look sophisticated, and because the people commissioning the dashboards are not thinking about the Cleveland and McGill accuracy hierarchy. The appearance of specialized sophistication is itself a decorative feature , one that impedes accurate reading.

segments.label: 07 / 13 · WHEN SPECIALIZED CHARTS BECOME

title: WHAT YOU CAN
NOW DO.
segments.body_html:
You can build candlestick and OHLC charts for financial data, encoding four values per period using position throughout,

What you can now do — You can build Kagi and Point and Figure charts for time-independent price analysis — trend reversals, support and resist

beats.text: You can build candlestick and OHLC charts for financial data, encoding four values per period using position throughout, and you understand the graphicacy prerequisite that makes the convention legible. You can build Kagi and Point and Figure charts for time-independent price analysis , trend reversals, support and resistance , and explain what question the time-independence serves and for whom.

segments.label: 08 / 13 · WHAT YOU CAN NOW DO

beats.text: Exercise 15.1 , Specialized form selection. (Tests: the earn-your-strangeness test) For each scenario below, apply the test ("what specific analytical question does this form answer better than any standard alternative?") and name the right form , or name the standard form if no specialized form earns its use: Daily stock price tracking with intraday volatility for a financially literate trading audience. A single performance metric vs. target on an executive dashboard. Multi-dimensional product comparison across 5 attributes for a general marketing audience.

title: AI WAYBACK
MACHINE.
segments.body_html: Munehisa Homma→Run this:→"Munehisa Homma"→Now make the prompt better.

beats.text: The ideas in this chapter didn't appear from nowhere. Munehisa Homma was an 18th-century Japanese rice trader who developed the candlestick charting technique to track price action in the Osaka rice futures market , the earliest organized derivatives exchange. His method reached the West only in the 1990s. Munehisa Homma, 18th century. AI-generated illustration based on a public domain painting (Wikimedia Commons).
